title: Keeping His First Wife Now
content:
آ آ آ آ It's oh! for a rivet in marriage bonds,
آ آ آ آ And a splice in the knot untied,
آ آ آ آ The sanctity of the marriage tie
آ آ آ آ Is growing more sanctified!
آ آ آ آ Theyï؟½re getting mixed up in society,
آ آ آ آ Thereï؟½s an awful family row,
آ آ آ آ For Reginald Jones of ï؟½The Ferneryï؟½
آ آ آ آ Is ï؟½keepingï؟½ his first wife now!

آ آ آ آ Oh! she belonged to the smart, smart set
آ آ آ آ (Where reasons are far to seek),
آ آ آ آ And the wedding and ï؟½crushï؟½ are remembered yet
آ آ آ آ As the ï؟½smartï؟½ things of the week.
آ آ آ آ Never an atom of love had she,
آ آ آ آ But they had a child somehow,
آ آ آ آ And Reginald Jones of ï؟½The Ferneryï؟½
آ آ آ آ Has the love of his first wife now.

آ آ آ آ Mad for ï؟½noticeï؟½ and ï؟½talkï؟½ was she,
آ آ آ آ A butterfly blind as a bat,
آ آ آ آ She would flaunt for a season a divorcee,
آ آ آ آ Or divorce him, failing that.
آ آ آ آ He played his part and she held his heart
آ آ آ آ As light as her marriage vow,
آ آ آ آ But Reginald Jones of ï؟½The Ferneryï؟½
آ آ آ آ Has a hold on his first wife now.

آ آ آ آ She swore in Court what the world knew false,
آ آ آ آ With never a thought of shame,
آ آ آ آ She was free to flaunt to her heartï؟½s content,
آ آ آ آ But she found it mighty tame:
آ آ آ آ The talk of the ï؟½townï؟½ for a week or two,
آ آ آ آ The gush, the smirk and the bow,
آ آ آ آ But Reginald Jones of ï؟½The Ferneryï؟½
آ آ آ آ Is the God of his first wife now!

آ آ آ آ Her soul grew sick of the smart, smart set,
آ آ آ آ Or her conscience drove her wild,
آ آ آ آ Or she craved for ï؟½noticeï؟½ and ï؟½talkï؟½ once more,
آ آ آ آ Or perhaps because of the child;
آ آ آ آ But they met at last and they met again,
آ آ آ آ No matter the where or how,
آ آ آ آ And Reginald Jones of ï؟½The Ferneryï؟½
آ آ آ آ Is in love with his first wife now.

آ آ آ آ ï؟½Tis a ï؟½terrible lifeï؟½ for the second young wife,
آ آ آ آ But she married him too for ï؟½placeï؟½;
آ آ آ آ And she mustnï؟½t forget that a smarting set
آ آ آ آ Belongs to the human race.
آ آ آ آ They say itï؟½s fixed up in camera,
آ آ آ آ And, if that is the case, Iï؟½ll vow,
آ آ آ آ That Reginald Jones of ï؟½The Ferneryï؟½
آ آ آ آ Will marry his first wife now.

آ آ آ آ And there is a song of the English world
آ آ آ آ And a song for the English race:
آ آ آ آ The second husband and second wife
آ آ آ آ Must ever take second place.
آ آ آ آ So cherish the best that you find in the first,
آ آ آ آ And a margin of width allow:
آ آ آ آ The future looks after itself too well!
آ آ آ آ Look after the first ones now.
meaning: About: Henry Lawson (17 June 1867 - 2 September 1922) was an Australian writer and poet. Along with his contemporary Banjo Paterson, Lawson is among the best-known Australian poets and fiction writers of the colonial period.
